This movie is basically an homage to Heat, right down to the running gun fight in traffic, vs just being a remake per se. Tough cop and tough bank robber with a tough crew go head to head. They attempt to give us some reason to care about characters with the usual fare (tough cop getting divorced and misses his kids, drinks too much, yada, yada) and is generally well done with a solid cast, with a few eye rolling moments that knock down what could have been a really good movie. I expected 50 cent to be terrible, but he actually does a decent job of it.  Dialogue is ok, some scenes are tense and taught, and it's an overall fun action movie, but Heat or Sicario it aint... But it's not a bad action flick, just not one you'll be talking about the next day or thinking about past seeing it. B-

But when it comes to military trained combat veterans against the police. Well, yeah, we all know who wins in reality. Especially when a soldier gets shot and killed by a police issue pistol that goes through a bullet proof vest. Military professionals are trained to kill and not to be killed. Yeah, that part of the movie makes it fall down many levels. Unfortunately.

By the time I made it about 45 seconds into the movie, I knew some kind of serious morons were involved, super serious morons. For those wondering HOW serious, these are the kind of morons who can't even use a calculator.....

2400 times a year, 44 times a week, 9 times a day, every 48 minutes....blah blah blah. Any competent 3rd grader should easily recognize the fact that "every 48 minutes" would be far more than 9x a day, and I only point that out in particular since it's the easiest to see being wrong...

2400 / 52 = 46.15 times a week = 6.6 times a day = 218 minutes......honestly it might be nitpicking, but FML if you've got your movie opening with something so obviously retarded then what the hell do you want from us? I realize maybe they expect average movie-goers to have sub-par intelligence, but this is just insulting.....

shame the writers of this movie couldnt make Gerard Butlers character make sense.
Botched his backstory so hard. They kidnap the driver and have a bunch of strippers over while he is decked out, when the fuck did the text his wife that, and why? It just doesn't make sense. Then he goes to find her a few days later, and she has a new dude already? So she is taking the moral high ground when she is already planning on leaving him and is already with someone else.
I didn't know this was a marriage drama, thought this was a movie about cops trying to prevent thieves from taking stuff.
They should have done his backstory way differently. Either show him actually cheating on his wife instead of him not doing it and it magically happens somehow and remove the other dude she was with when he went to find her. He doesn't serve any purpose for the story other than to make his wife look like a shit person. I know there are a lot of characters but so far 1h in Gerards character is the only one with a semblance of a backstory and they fucked it up so bad.

I wonder if there is a limit on how original human beings can be. How many ideas can they come up with until they run out of new ones and then just recycle old ones. I think the past decade or so has shown that this is what seems to be happening to the movie industry, in my opinion. For example, this trailer really bored me. I didn't finish it. For me, the last original movie I saw was The Matrix. I think for a movie to be original it starts with an original idea that people have not thought of before and then the script and movie is made to enhance that idea and make it awesome. The end result should be an original movie that blows people away. Actually now that i think about it The Matrix should not have been that original to people who came before me. After all the books and movies it was based on (Neuromancer, Alice's adventures in wonderland, Bladerunner, etc.) came well before it was made. So I guess even The Matrix is based on "recycled" ideas. The question then becomes how recycled. Is the idea different enough that it can be considered original.

Took a class on this, focused on Film. Essentially every thing we have created has certain themes present in all. A lot of modern story telling has elements of the Odyssey and other stories we have recorded from that time. You're correct in suggesting that most ideas are recycled in some way.
Better come with it to compete with "Heat"

Very good point. I think, then, that if we combine ideas or try and modify them a little to make something different and a bit more original would be the best way forward. I think this is applicable to all art forms: novels, computer games, music, etc. The way I see it is that there are fundamental ideas and then derived ideas that can be built on top of those fundamentals and if presented well can lead to something that appears original - I have never studied any of this, I'm just expressing my thoughts :). But also a very important thing to consider, I think, is the person watching the movie in this case. What is their movie viewing history/experience? Have they seen something like that before? How old is the person watching? Have they been exposed to similar ideas before and hence may find the movie, novel, etc. boring? What are the person's interests? There are quite a lot of factors that may determine whether someone will like something or not. In the case of this movie I personally find it boring but, of course, that does not give me the right to judge or critique others for liking it. Everyone has unique tastes based on their experience, personality, etc. I think that if someone enjoys this trailer they should definitely go see the movie.
